steps.title: Spot the overlap signal
steps.content: The passage states: “for every class of animals examined, there were individual studies showing differences well above the average for arthropods.” This single statement reveals that individual bird studies exceeded the overall arthropod average—creating an overlap between the two groups.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
If individual bird studies exceed the arthropod average, they must surpass some arthropod studies (specifically those below the average), which is exactly what D states.
  • A contradicts the average relationship
  • B makes an unsubstantiated claim about a specific study
  • C introduces information the passage never addresses
.

This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
